description: Linux/Ubuntu VS Code cleanup: detects install method (apt | snap | deb | tarball | user-config) and removes ONLY the matching files, packages, and config — never enumerative.

messages.scriptDisabled: Script disabled in config.json (set 'enabled': true to run).
messages.wrongOs: This script targets Linux only. Detected OS: {os}. Aborting (no changes made).
messages.needsSudo: [{method}] step '{kind}' for '{target}' requires sudo. Re-run with sudo, or pass --scope user. SKIPPING.
messages.modeApply: APPLY mode: changes will be made. Pass --dry-run to preview only.
messages.modeDryRun: DRY-RUN mode: no changes will be made.
messages.summary: Cleanup summary -- removed: {removed}, would-remove: {would}, missing: {missing}, skipped: {skipped}, failed: {failed} (mode={mode}, scope={scope}).
messages.noMethods: No VS Code install methods detected on this machine. Nothing to do.
messages.rerunWithDryRun: Tip: re-run with --dry-run first to preview the exact set of packages/paths that will be removed.
messages.ctxScanFail: Context-menu/MIME scan found {count} VS Code wiring entry(ies) still present after cleanup. See {path}
messages.ctxScanPass: Context-menu/MIME scan: no VS Code wiring detected on any inspected surface.
